claim_text: Archaea Energy, our renewable natural gas business, purchased renewable energy certificates equivalent to 125ktCO2e in Scope 2 emissions savings on a market basis.
rationale_note: Claim contains a specific quantified figure of 125ktCO2e and a defined Scope 2 emissions savings mechanism via renewable energy certificates linked to Archaea Energy. This is a concrete, testable claim; evidence does not address or contradict this specific transaction.

claim_text: In 2025 the number of reported oil spills increased to 110 compared with 96 in 2024 and 100 in 2023.
rationale_note: Claim is a specific measurable report of oil spill counts across 2023 to 2025, with exact yearly figures (110, 96, 100). This is testable operational data, not vague intent or aspiration. Evidence does not address or contradict these spill numbers.

claim_text: Our aim is to support biodiversity where we operate, by: Our aim is to reduce our net freshwater use in stressed catchments where we operate, by: • Aiming to achieve net positive impact (NPI) • Being more efficient with freshwater use on all new in-scope projects.
rationale_note: Claim uses bundled aspirational language like “Our aim is to support biodiversity” and “aiming to achieve net positive impact” and “being more efficient” with no concrete numeric target, deadline, or clearly testable scope tied to a measurable commitment.

claim_text: Our 2023-25 long-term incentive plan scorecard was linked to emissions reductions against our 2019 baseline (15%).
rationale_note: Claim is specific and testable with a 2023–25 incentive plan, 15% emissions reduction metric, and 2019 baseline. It defines scope through the scorecard mechanism. Evidence does not address or contradict this structure; classification reflects support-by-absence rather than independent verification.

claim_text: In 2025 we focused on delivering two green hydrogen projects sanctioned in 2024: our 50:50 JV with Iberdrola at our Castellón refinery in Spain, and next to our refinery in Lingen, Germany.
rationale_note: Claim is a descriptive activity statement about focusing on delivering two previously sanctioned green hydrogen projects (Castellón JV with Iberdrola and Lingen refinery project). It reports execution focus rather than a concrete measurable target or commitment, so it is not testable as a specific outcome.

claim_text: And the Tangguh UCC project in Indonesia, which we have invested in with partners, and will include development of the country’s first at-scale enhanced gas recovery project through carbon capture, utilization and storage.
rationale_note: Claim is specific and testable; it names the Tangguh UCC project in Indonesia, describes investment with partners, and states it will include at-scale enhanced gas recovery using carbon capture, utilization and storage. Evidence is general fossil fuel and climate commentary and does not address or contradict this specific project.

claim_text: Completed abatement projects from 2021 to 2024 designed to abate 1.2 million tonnes per year of carbon dioxide-equivalent (CO2e) emissions.
rationale_note: Claim is specific and testable; it states abatement projects completed from 2021–2024 designed to reduce 1.2 million tonnes per year of CO2e emissions. Evidence is general climate and fossil fuel reporting and does not address or contradict this quantified abatement figure.

claim_text: The associated decrease in fuel usage and flaring of excess gas is anticipated to reduce greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by approximately 26,000 tonnes.
rationale_note: Claim is specific and testable; it states an anticipated reduction of approximately 26,000 tonnes of GHG emissions from reduced fuel use and flaring. Evidence is general industry commentary on emissions and policy and does not address or contradict this specific quantified estimate.

claim_text: Invested in SwissDrones, a startup developing uncrewed helicopters for surveillance, emissions detection, asset inspection and cargo delivery.
rationale_note: Claim describes an investment in SwissDrones, a named startup and its use cases. It is a past activity statement without a quantified amount, timeline, or defined investment scope; therefore not testable under the rule set.

claim_text: We aim to grow our oil and gas business, lower the carbon intensity of our operations and grow new businesses in renewable fuels, carbon capture and offsets, hydrogen, power generation for data centers, and emerging technologies.
rationale_note: Claim is a bundled set of broad strategic intentions including growing oil and gas, reducing carbon intensity, and expanding into multiple low-carbon areas such as renewable fuels, carbon capture, hydrogen, and data center power. It contains no numeric targets, deadlines, or defined measurable scope; therefore not testable under the rule set.

claim_text: Venezuela NOJV emissions have not been included for emissions reporting since 2021.
rationale_note: Claim states Venezuela NOJV emissions have been excluded from emissions reporting since 2021, a specific reporting boundary and timeframe; evidence does not address this reporting treatment, so support-by-absence rather than contradiction.

claim_text: reducing carbon intensity approximately 71,000 tonnes annually of CO2e emissions over the remaining operating life of the equipment.
rationale_note: Claim provides a quantified emissions reduction of approximately 71,000 tonnes of CO2e annually over the remaining operating life of the equipment, including a time-bound operational lifecycle framing. Retrieved evidence discusses broader fossil fuel emissions and carbon capture debates but does not directly address or contradict this specific quantified reduction claim.

claim_text: In 2025, operations in the Asia Pacific segment contributed four percent of our consolidated liquids production and two percent of our consolidated natural gas production.
rationale_note: Claim contains specific quantitative breakdown of production share by region and fuel type in 2025, stating Asia Pacific contributes 4% of consolidated liquids production and 2% of natural gas production. This is a testable, scoped operational distribution claim; retrieved evidence is general and does not address or contradict these figures.

claim_text: The LNG is being sold to Sinopec under a 20-year sales agreement for 7.6 MTPA of LNG, and Japan-based Kansai Electric Power Co., Inc. under a 20-year sales agreement for approximately one MTPA of LNG.
rationale_note: Claim contains specific testable details including named counterparties (Sinopec and Kansai Electric Power), contract duration (20-year agreements), and LNG volumes (7.6 MTPA and ~1 MTPA). These are concrete, measurable trade terms, so the claim is testable; retrieved evidence does not address or contradict these specific contractual figures.

claim_text: In 2025, we invested $12.6 billion in capital expenditures and investments, $0.5 billion of which was primarily payments towards our equity investments in LNG projects, including NFE4, NFS3 and PALNG.
rationale_note: Claim contains multiple concrete, testable anchors: $12.6 billion total capital expenditures in 2025, and $0.5 billion allocated to LNG equity investments including NFE4, NFS3, and PALNG; retrieved evidence does not address or contradict these specific investment figures or project allocations.

claim_text: As of April 2024, the British Columbia Output Based Pricing System (BC OBPS) regulation requires facilities or linear operations (such as oil and gas gathering systems) with emissions equal to or greater than 10,000 metric tonnes of carbon dioxide or equivalent per year to remit payments on the difference between actual emissions and allowable emissions based on product and activity benchmarks.
rationale_note: Claim states that as of April 2024 the BC Output Based Pricing System requires facilities with emissions ≥10,000 tCO2e per year to remit payments based on the gap between actual and benchmark emissions; it includes a specific jurisdiction, threshold, and compliance mechanism. Retrieved evidence does not address or contradict this regulatory description.

claim_text: Delivered Lower 48 drilling and completion efficiencies >15% year over year.
rationale_note: Claim contains a clear measurable anchor with “>15%” efficiency improvement and a defined operational scope (Lower 48 drilling and completion efficiencies). It also has clear attribution to the company’s operations; evidence does not address or contradict this specific performance statement.

claim_text: At December 31, 2025, our balance sheet included total accrued environmental costs of $220 million, compared with $206 million at December 31, 2024, for remediation activities in the U.S. and Canada.
rationale_note: Claim provides specific financial figures for accrued environmental costs, $220 million at Dec 31, 2025 compared with $206 million at Dec 31, 2024, and specifies scope as remediation activities in the U.S. and Canada. Retrieved evidence does not address or contradict these accounting figures or their scope.

claim_text: SAF production in Gela: inaugurated the first plant dedicated to Sustainable Aviation Fuel, with a capacity of 400 ktonnes/y, equal to approximately one-third of the European demand expected for 2025, in line with the ReFuelEU Aviation regulation.
rationale_note: Claim is specific and testable; it states inauguration of a Sustainable Aviation Fuel plant in Gela with 400 ktonnes per year capacity, alongside a quantified share of expected European demand and reference to ReFuelEU Aviation regulation. Evidence does not address or contradict this facility or its stated capacity, so classification reflects support-by-absence rather than independent verification.

claim_text: In 2024, extensions and new discoveries of 14 mmbbl were mainly the result of the inclusion of reserves from the Coral North project.
rationale_note: Claim is specific and testable; it states 2024 reserve additions and extensions of 14 mmbbl, mainly attributed to inclusion of reserves from the Coral North project. Retrieved evidence does not address or contradict this specific reserves accounting statement.

claim_text: An integral component of the Company’s strategic plan, defining net GHG emissions-reduction targets, levers and allocated resources, with a view to transitioning towards a lower carbon economy, contributing to limiting global warming to 1.5°C and achieving Eni’s Carbon Neutrality by 2050.
rationale_note: Claim is a high-level strategic statement about net GHG reduction targets, transition to a lower carbon economy, and achieving carbon neutrality by 2050 with alignment to 1.5°C goals; it lacks specific numeric target values, defined scope boundaries, or measurable intermediate commitments, so it is not independently testable under the rule set.

claim_text: Enilive engages in the manufacturing of biofuels from renewable raw materials and in retail marketing activities of traditional and biofuels through an extensive network of refueling outlets, also providing non-fuel products and services to drivers with a view to sustainable mobility.
rationale_note: Claim describes Enilive’s business activities in biofuel production from renewable feedstocks, retail distribution of traditional and biofuels, and related mobility services. It is a descriptive operational statement and not a numeric or time-bound target. Evidence does not address or contradict these activities.

claim_text: Compared to the previous reporting period, there is an increase in the number of operational sites across non‑Oil & Gas Upstream business lines overlapping with protected areas and KBAs (39, sites compared to 28 in 2024), mainly due to new Plenitude acquisitions.
rationale_note: Claim is testable with clear structure: comparison across reporting periods (28 sites in 2024 vs 39 sites currently), defined scope (operational sites in non-Oil & Gas Upstream business lines), and a named driver (Plenitude acquisitions). Evidence is general climate and fossil fuel context and does not address or contradict this specific biodiversity exposure metric.

claim_text: Eni intends to use carbon credits only after reducing 90‑95% of its GHG emissions to achieve the 2050 Net Zero target for Net Intensity Scope 1+2+3.
rationale_note: Claim is specific and testable; it includes a quantified reduction pathway of 90–95% GHG emissions before using carbon credits and a defined 2050 Net Zero target covering Scope 1+2+3 net intensity. Retrieved evidence does not address or contradict this credit-use hierarchy or target structure.

claim_text: The European Council and Parliament reached a provisional agreement on a 90% emissions-reduction target for 2040, including the potential use of international credits from 2036 onward.
rationale_note: Claim describes an EU provisional agreement on a 90% emissions reduction target for 2040 with potential international credit use from 2036; it has numbers and timelines but lacks Equinor as an actor, making it a policy description rather than a company-specific testable claim.

claim_text: Indirect emissions from purchased energy, including electricity, steam, heating and cooling (scope 2) constitute 0.03%-0.4% of total GHG emissions dependent on reporting methodology (location- vs. market-based approach).
rationale_note: Claim provides a quantified range (0.03%–0.4%) of total GHG emissions attributed to Scope 2 indirect emissions from purchased energy, with a defined methodological variation (location-based vs market-based); this makes it testable. Retrieved evidence does not address or contradict these specific emission proportions or methodology.

claim_text: In 2025 the total energy consumption from own operations was 34 TWh, a change of -3% compared to 2024.
rationale_note: Claim is specific and testable; it includes a quantified energy use figure of 34 TWh for 2025 and a -3% year-on-year change compared to 2024, both of which are measurable operational metrics tied to Equinor’s own operations; retrieved evidence does not address or contradict this reported energy consumption change.

claim_text: In March a final investment decision was taken on the Northern Lights expansion project (Phase 2), which will increase transport and storage capacity to a minimum of 5 million tonnes CO2 per year.
rationale_note: Claim is attributable to Equinor and states a final investment decision on Northern Lights expansion Phase 2 in March, with a defined capacity increase to at least 5 million tonnes of CO2 per year. Evidence does not address or contradict this specific project or capacity figure.

claim_text: the target of a maximum 6 leakages was met.
rationale_note: Claim states a target of a maximum 6 leakages was met, but it is incomplete and ambiguous as written; it lacks context for what “leakages” refers to, what system or scope it applies to, and provides no clear measurable framing or attribution beyond a fragmented outcome statement, making it not fully testable as a structured company claim.

claim_text: Approximately 69% of Equinor’s expected oil and gas reserves are planned to be produced in the period 2026-2035, and around 96% in the period 2026-2050.
rationale_note: Claim is specific and testable; it provides quantified proportions of Equinor’s expected oil and gas reserves production across two defined time periods (2026–2035 at ~69% and 2026–2050 at ~96%). Evidence is general fossil fuel reserve and climate commentary and does not address or contradict these specific production distribution figures.

claim_text: Our Advancing Climate Solutions report details our approach to reducing emissions.
rationale_note: Claim states that Exxon’s “Advancing Climate Solutions” report describes its approach to reducing emissions. This is a reference to a report or communication rather than a measurable commitment, target, or quantified outcome, and it contains no number, timeline, scope, or baseline.

claim_text: In late 2024, we announced plans to invest more than $200 million to expand advanced recycling operations in Baytown and Beaumont, Texas.
rationale_note: Claim has clear testable anchors including a late 2024 timeframe, a quantified investment of more than $200 million, and specific project locations in Baytown and Beaumont, Texas. It describes a defined capital allocation to expand advanced recycling operations. Retrieved evidence discusses general plastics industry concerns but does not address or contradict this specific investment plan.

claim_text: This first-of-its-kind plastic waste processing facility will have the capacity to produce 300 million pounds of plastic feedstock per year to be used in both advanced and mechanical recycling of post-consumer, commercial, and industrial plastic waste.
rationale_note: Claim describes a specific plastic waste processing facility with a stated capacity of 300 million pounds per year of plastic feedstock for recycling pathways. It contains a measurable output figure and defined operational scope, making it testable; evidence does not address or contradict this specific facility.

claim_text: In 2024, we announced a $200 million investment to expand our advanced recycling capacity to 500 million pounds.
rationale_note: Claim includes specific, testable figures: a $200 million investment, a 2024 announcement, and a capacity expansion target to 500 million pounds for advanced recycling. Evidence is general criticism of advanced recycling feasibility and does not address or contradict these specific figures; classification reflects support-by-absence rather than independent verification.

claim_text: A recent U.S.-based study found that polyethylene (PE) packaging - the most widely used plastic packaging - can reduce lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions by up to 70% compared to alternative materials like glass, paper, and aluminum.
rationale_note: Claim relays findings from a U.S.-based study on polyethylene packaging lifecycle emissions reductions up to 70% versus alternative materials; it is third-party research being cited rather than a company commitment or performance claim.

claim_text: ExxonMobil expects to increase the amount of recycled produced water used in its Permian Basin hydraulic fracturing operations to more than 90% by 2030.
rationale_note: Claim states ExxonMobil expects to increase recycled produced water use in Permian Basin hydraulic fracturing operations to more than 90% by 2030. It includes a clear target (90%) and deadline (2030) with a defined operational scope (Permian Basin hydraulic fracturing). Retrieved evidence does not address this specific water reuse target or contradict it, so classification reflects absence of direct verification rather than support.

claim_text: Our centralized Power Team works to provide affordable, reliable power delivery across our North American assets, including the daily management of three cogeneration facilities, a solar plant and retail consumption across 16 states and in Canada.
rationale_note: Claim describes operational activity and infrastructure management (power team managing cogeneration facilities, a solar plant, and retail consumption across regions). It has no target, no numerical performance metric, no deadline, and no emissions or investment threshold, so it is not a testable commitment under the rules.

claim_text: This could give our sequestration hubs a collective capacity to sequester up to 6 billion metric tons of CO2.
rationale_note: Claim states sequestration hubs could have a collective capacity to sequester up to 6 billion metric tons of CO2. It includes a quantified figure but is framed as potential capacity (“could give”, “up to”), indicating uncertainty rather than a firm delivered target. Evidence does not address or contradict this specific capacity estimate.

claim_text: We achieved ZRF in our U.S. oil and gas operations in 2022, eight years ahead of target, and are on track to reach the 2030 target across our operations.
rationale_note: Claim includes specific past achievement of ZRF in 2022 and a forward statement about being on track for a 2030 target. Evidence does not address or contradict either element. Claim is testable but mixes verified past performance with forward-looking progress framing.

claim_text: of unconventional EOR using captured CO2 is a key part of Oxy’s Net-Zero Strategy.
rationale_note: Claim is a partial sentence stating CO2-based enhanced oil recovery is part of Oxy’s Net-Zero Strategy. It is framed as a strategic description without a complete measurable commitment or standalone testable target, and thus not independently verifiable as a specific claim.

claim_text: Oxy also assesses methane intensity using the Natural Gas Sustainability Initiative (NGSI) method, which divides estimated methane emissions attributed solely to gas production by our operated wet gas production.
rationale_note: Claim describes Occidental’s methane intensity calculation methodology using NGSI (emissions divided by operated wet gas production). It is a measurement approach rather than a target or quantified performance outcome, so it is not independently testable as a commitment.

claim_text: In 2024, Oxy joined Pipeline Research Council International which seeks to enhance the safety, reliability and productivity of the energy pipeline industry.
rationale_note: Claim states Occidental joined Pipeline Research Council International in 2024, an industry body focused on pipeline safety and reliability. It describes participation in an organization rather than a measurable ESG target or performance outcome, so it is not a testable claim.

claim_text: The construction of both projects, which are integrated into strategic industrial clusters and will contribute significantly to the decarbonization of industry and transport, has already been approved, and they are expected to enter into operation in 2029.
rationale_note: Claim describes two approved Repsol projects expected to begin operation in 2029. It includes a clear timeline and defined project scope, making it testable. Retrieved evidence does not address or contradict these specific projects.

claim_text: Impact on global warming of Repsol’s activities in the areas of Upstream and Industrial, as well as those related to mobility, which generate Scope 1 and 2 (direct company operations) and Scope 3 (mobility and supply chain) emissions.
rationale_note: Claim describes Repsol’s operational emissions footprint across Upstream, Industrial, and mobility activities, covering Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions. It defines emission sources rather than stating a target, reduction outcome, or measurable performance change.

claim_text: In July, Repsol announced an investment of €16 million in the Puertollano industrial complex, with the aim of producing 5,300 tons per year of renewable hydrogen from bio-based gas (produced from waste), replacing natural gas.
rationale_note: Claim describes a €16 million investment in Repsol’s Puertollano industrial complex to produce 5,300 tons per year of renewable hydrogen from bio-based gas, replacing natural gas. It contains a specific investment value, production target, and defined technology pathway, making it a testable project-level claim. Retrieved evidence does not address or contradict this specific project.

claim_text: The C1 scenarios envisage a global reduction in greenhouse gas emissions of 40–50% in 2030 compared with 2019, with steeper declines in coal (≈ 75%) and smaller declines in oil and gas (≈ 10%).
rationale_note: Claim describes Repsol C1 scenario projections for global emissions reductions (40–50% by 2030 vs 2019 with sectoral splits). It is a modeled pathway (“envisage”) rather than a company action or commitment, so it is not a testable performance claim.

claim_text: Meanwhile, the CII stood at 65.2 gCO₂e/MJ, which represents a decline of 15.1% compared with 2016, exceeding the target of –15% in 2025 and with a focus on achieving a reduction of 25% ±1 in 2030.
rationale_note: Claim reports CII emissions intensity of 65.2 gCO₂e/MJ, showing a 15.1% reduction vs 2016, exceeding a –15% 2025 target and stating a –25% ±1 target for 2030. It includes clear baseline, percentages, and forward targets, making it fully testable. Retrieved evidence does not address or contradict these specific figures.

claim_text: Specifically, actions 1.1 and 1.2 contribute to the target of reaching a production volume of between 125,000 and 146,000 tons by 2030 of circular or bio-based products that incorporate alternative raw materials such as waste plastic or other renewable materials.
rationale_note: Claim defines a specific production target of 125,000–146,000 tons of circular or bio-based products by 2030 linked to actions 1.1 and 1.2. It is a measurable and time-bound target, and retrieved evidence does not address or contradict this specific commitment.

claim_text: In 2025, we disposed of 1,987 thousand tonnes of waste, compared with 1,933 thousand tonnes in 2024.
rationale_note: Claim reports Shell’s waste disposal volumes in 2025 (1,987 thousand tonnes) compared with 2024 (1,933 thousand tonnes). It is a quantitative operational disclosure with clear time comparison and scope. Retrieved evidence does not address or contradict waste disposal figures.

claim_text: Storage levels comfortably reached the EU mandatory targets by October 2025.
rationale_note: Claim states storage levels reached EU mandatory targets by October 2025. It is framed as an outcome statement but lacks clear numeric target definition or measurable benchmark in the claim text. It reads as a reported compliance outcome without specifying scope or baseline, and evidence does not address or verify this specific storage compliance assertion.

claim_text: Our standards require new assets and existing assets modified after July 1, 2024, to use best available techniques (BAT) and meet best environmental practice (BEP) assessment criteria for water treatment.
rationale_note: Claim describes Shell’s internal standards requiring new and modified assets (post July 1, 2024) to apply BAT and meet BEP criteria for water treatment. It is a policy requirement statement without a quantified target, outcome, or performance result, so it is not a testable emissions or environmental performance claim.

claim_text: Revised from 293 tonnes to 402 tonnes for 2024 due to a reporting omission.
rationale_note: Claim reports a revision of emissions figures for 2024 from 293 to 402 tonnes due to a reporting omission. It is a data correction statement rather than a forward-looking target or performance commitment, and the evidence does not address this specific adjustment.

claim_text: In September 2025, Google selected Shell as its renewable energy manager in the UK, leveraging Shell's trading and battery capabilities to support Google's decarbonisation goals.
rationale_note: Claim describes Google selecting Shell as renewable energy manager in the UK, referencing a commercial partnership and service role rather than a Shell-led emissions target or measurable ESG commitment. Evidence does not address this specific arrangement.

claim_text: We have set targets to reduce our Scope 1 and 2 emissions and our net carbon intensity (NCI), as well as an ambition to reduce customer emissions from the use of our oil products (Scope 3, Category 11).
rationale_note: Claim states that Shell has set targets for Scope 1 and 2 emissions and net carbon intensity and expresses ambition to reduce Scope 3 emissions from product use. It describes the existence of targets but does not specify numerical values, timelines, or baselines, so it is not a testable commitment.

claim_text: over 10 Mt in 2024
rationale_note: Claim is an incomplete numeric fragment (“over 10 Mt in 2024”) with no defined metric, scope, or action. It is not a full, testable ESG commitment or reported outcome.

claim_text: TotalEnergies has been a very active contributor to the Oil & Gas Decarbonization Charter (OGDC) initiative since its creation at the end of 2023.
rationale_note: Claim describes TotalEnergies’ participation as a “very active contributor” to the Oil & Gas Decarbonization Charter since 2023. It reports involvement in an industry initiative rather than a measurable target or performance outcome.

claim_text: The investment supports Improved Forest Management (IFM) practices across a portfolio of 20 carbon projects, covering 300,000 hectares in 10 states across the United States.
rationale_note: Claim describes TotalEnergies’ investment supporting Improved Forest Management across 20 carbon projects covering 300,000 hectares in 10 U.S. states. It includes clear scale and geographic scope, and retrieved evidence does not address or contradict this specific portfolio.

claim_text: Reducing GHG emissions at our operated facilities (Scope 1+2) is key to our ambition to supply more energy while curbing GHG emissions.
rationale_note: Claim frames reducing Scope 1+2 emissions as part of company ambition. It is a strategic intent statement with no target, number, or deadline, so it is not testable as a performance claim.

claim_text: targeting a capacity of 12 GW by 2030, with more than 8 already identified.
rationale_note: Claim sets a specific renewable capacity target of 12 GW by 2030 with partial pipeline already identified (8+ GW). It is a measurable forward-looking commitment with clear capacity and timeline, and retrieved evidence does not address or contradict this target.

claim_text: The Company will put more than ten projects into production portfolio combining renewable (solar, onshore wind, offshore wind) and flexible assets (CCGT, storage) to deliver low-carbon electricity available 24/7.
rationale_note: Claim describes a planned portfolio of more than ten renewable and flexible energy projects aimed at delivering 24/7 low-carbon electricity. It is a bundled forward-looking statement without a clear deadline, measurable performance threshold, or defined KPI, so it is not strictly testable as a commitment.
